channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power has been restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv, a day after Russia launched major attacks on Ukraine's power grid, causing widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to visit Egypt and Israel early next week amid ceasefire deal in Middle East
item.description: President Trump said Friday he plans to visit Egypt and Israel during a one-day trip to the Middle East early next week to mark the implementation of a peace agreement that will see a pause in fighting in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday for her struggle to achieve a democratic transition in the South American nation, winning recognition as a woman “who keeps the flame of democracy burning amid a growing darkness.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, REGINA GARCIA CANO and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sends CENTCOM troops to Israel for Trump-brokered Hamas ceasefire monitoring
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said U.S. Central Command troops will establish a ceasefire monitoring center in Israel as the President Donald Trump-brokered peace deal between Israel and Hamas begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts despite failing to win the Nobel prize
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump's peace efforts, despite not winning a Nobel, and voiced hope for securing an agreement to extend a pivotal nuclear arms pact with Washington for another year.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: PHOTOS: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump in talks with Putin on reuniting Ukrainian children with families
item.description: First lady Melania Trump said Friday she has been in communication with Russian President Vladimir Putin about reuniting Ukrainian children displaced by war with their families.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize chair responds to Trump's pressure campaign
item.description: The chair of the Nobel Peace Prize committee responded Friday to President Trump's public pressure campaign to win the award, telling reporters that the deliberation process for choosing the awardee prioritizes courage and integrity.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: The Taliban government blames Pakistan for a strike on Kabul
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The Taliban government on Friday blamed Pakistan for striking the Afghan capital and bombing a market in the country's east, accusing its neighbor of violating national airspace. A blast struck before 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel's military says Gaza ceasefire now in effect
item.description: Israel's military announced early Friday that the ceasefire in Gaza is now in effect, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet approved the first phase of President Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians are starting to walk north to return to their homes after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that troops were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Citing unease over graft, Japan's Komeito leaves the longstanding ruling coalition headed by the LDP
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The head of Japan's Komeito says it is leaving the ruling coalition headed by the Liberal Democratic Party due to concerns over corruption, in a major setback for the woman who hopes to become the country's next prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doesn’t get Nobel Peace Prize; María Corina Machado awarded
item.description: President Trump failed in his bid to be awarded the Nobel Peace Prize, with the Norwegian Nobel committee announcing Friday that María Corina Machado was given this year’s award for promoting democratic rights for the people of Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: King Mohammed VI set to speak as Gen Z protesters demand reforms in Morocco
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco's elusive King Mohammed VI inaugurates parliament Friday in a speech with far higher stakes than in recent years, as anti-government protests sweep the nation's cities, questioning the government's spending priorities.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peru’s Congress removes President Boluarte as a crime wave grips the country
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s Congress voted early Friday to remove deeply unpopular President Dina Boluarte from office as a crime wave grips the South American nation and quickly replaced her with 38-year-old lawyer José Jerí, the legislative body's leader.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron is set to appoint a new French prime minister in last-ditch move to tackle turmoil
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After a week of intense political turmoil, French President Emmanuel Macron is set to appoint a new prime minister Friday in his latest bid to break the political deadlock that has gripped the country for more than a year, as France struggles with mounting economic challenges and balloo
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows to build a 'socialist paradise' ahead of a likely military parade
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un lauded what he called his country's fight against a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Madagascar's Gen Z protesters clash with police as they call for president to step down
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — About 1,000 protesters marched in Madagascar's capital on Thursday, clashing with police who used tear gas and stun grenades to disperse them in the third week of what has been the most significant unrest in years in the Indian Ocean island nation.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TÉTAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombian President Petro suggests Qatar could mediate US military actions off Venezuela
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro proposed Thursday that Qatar could serve as “mediator” so that the United States would end its military deployment in international waters off the coast of Venezuela, where it has attacked small boats it says were carrying drugs.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Obama: We 'should all be encouraged' that end to Israel-Hamas war in sight
item.description: Former President Obama on Thursday praised the announcement of the first phase of a peace deal brokered between Israel and Hamas following two years of war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Trump expects Israeli hostages to be released on Monday
item.description: President Donald Trump says he expects the remaining hostages to be released from Gaza on Monday as part of the first phase of Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Peace celebrations in Gaza marred by daunting rebuilding phase to come
item.description: A freelance photographer based in Gaza details his hope for life in the war-torn city now that a ceasefire deal has been reached.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: From grief to gratitude: Trump praised as Israelis celebrate Hamas hostage deal in Tel Aviv
item.description: Israelis express joy, relief and grief at Hostages Square following President Donald Trump's announcement of agreement to end Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World leaders express hope after Israel and Hamas agreed to first phase of plan to end Gaza war
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — World leaders on Thursday expressed hope for peace and urged Israel and Hamas to fulfill their commitments in the hours after U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe takes on 'hybrid war' as Germany allows police to shoot down drones
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen warns Russia's 'hybrid warfare' targeting Europe through airspace violations and cyber attacks needs to be directly acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Before and after images show devastating destruction in Gaza
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals widespread devastation in Gaza cities, including Rafah and Beit Hanoun, since the start of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military intercepts another flotilla heading to Gaza, detains scores of activists
item.description: The ministry said the 145 activists, who were in good health, were being brought to shore in Israel for processing and are expected to be deported soon.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Priscilla nears major hurricane status in Pacific as tropical storm swirls in Atlantic
item.description: Just off the west coast of Mexico, Priscilla was spinning with maximum sustained winds around 105 mph and moving northwest at 9 mph.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuban-led caravan aims for Mexico City as Trump policies deter migrants from US
item.description: Cuban migrants in Mexico choose Mexico City over U.S. destinations as President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies influence regional migration trends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Snowstorm traps hundreds of hikers on Mount Everest during China's national holiday
item.description: About 350 hikers had reached a meeting point in Tingri country and rescuers were in contact with another 200, state broadcaster CCTV said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Israeli and Hamas officials discuss US peace plan for Gaza
item.description: Israeli and Hamas officials are holding indirect talks in an Egyptian resort Monday on a U.S. peace plan for Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Negotiators meet in Egypt seeking Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Talks are split into phases: first, securing the release of hostages, then tackling Hamas' demilitarization.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation
